SEARCH
Package app.client

Class ApiClient

java.lang.Object
app.client.ApiClient
public class ApiClient extends Object
Centralized HTTP client responsible for managing and sending API requests within the application. It implements the Singleton pattern to ensure a single, shared instance of the HTTP client and the JSON object mapper. *

The class configures a default HttpClient (HTTP/1.1 with a 10-second connection timeout) and a Jackson ObjectMapper optimized to support Java 8 Date and Time APIs.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static ApiClient
    getInstance()
    Returns the unique instance of ApiClient.
    HttpResponse<String>
    sendRequest(HttpRequest.Builder requestBuilder)
    Builds and sends an HTTP request synchronously, handles the response, manages logs, and processes any network or business logic exceptions.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Method Details

    • getInstance

      public static ApiClient getInstance()
      Returns the unique instance of ApiClient. If the instance does not exist yet, it is lazily created.
      Returns:
      The Singleton instance of ApiClient.

    • sendRequest

      public HttpResponse<String> sendRequest(HttpRequest.Builder requestBuilder) throws NetworkException, ApiException
      Builds and sends an HTTP request synchronously, handles the response, manages logs, and processes any network or business logic exceptions.
      Parameters:
      requestBuilder - The pre-configured HttpRequest.Builder containing the URI, parameters, and headers.
      Returns:
      An HttpResponse containing the server response as a String.
      Throws:
      NetworkException - If an I/O connection error occurs or if the request is interrupted.
      ApiException - If the server responds with an error status code (greater than or equal to 400).
